sections.heading: Protein Intake Calculator – Find Your Daily Protein Needs
sections.content: Our Protein Intake Calculator helps you determine exactly how much protein you need per day based on your weight, age, gender, activity level, and fitness goals. Whether you want to build muscle, lose fat, improve recovery, or maintain weight, this free tool provides a personalized daily protein requirement backed by nutrition science.

sections.heading: How Much Protein Do I Need Daily?

subSections.heading: Sedentary adults
subSections.content: 0.8 g of protein per kilogram of body weight.

subSections.heading: Example
subSections.content: A 70 kg person aiming for muscle gain may need 112–154 g of protein per day.

sections.heading: Recommended Protein Intake Calculator by Goal

subSections.heading: Muscle Gain
subSections.content: Higher protein (1.6–2.2 g/kg) to support hypertrophy and recovery.

subSections.heading: Fat Loss
subSections.content: Slightly higher protein (1.8–2.4 g/kg) to preserve lean muscle mass.

subSections.heading: Athletes & Runners
subSections.content: Moderate to high protein (1.2–2.0 g/kg) for endurance and recovery.

sections.heading: Protein Intake Chart by Body Weight

subSections.heading: Step 1
subSections.content: Enter your weight, age, gender, and activity level in the Protein Intake Calculator Online.

subSections.heading: Step 2
subSections.content: Choose your goal – muscle gain, fat loss, or maintenance.

subSections.heading: Step 3
subSections.content: Get your daily protein intake in grams, along with a recommended range.

sections.heading: Why Use a Protein Needs Calculator?

subSections.heading: AI-powered precision
subSections.content: Unlike generic charts, this tool adjusts for activity level, BMI, and fitness goals for a personalized result.

faq.q: What is my recommended protein intake?
faq.a: Your protein needs depend on body weight and activity level. Most adults need 0.8–2.2 g/kg/day depending on their fitness goals.

faq.q: How much protein per kg of body weight?
faq.a: Sedentary: 0.8 g/kg | Active: 1.2–2.0 g/kg | Muscle gain: 1.6–2.2 g/kg | Fat loss: 1.8–2.4 g/kg.

faq.q: Is high protein safe for fat loss?
faq.a: Yes, when paired with a calorie deficit. Higher protein helps preserve lean muscle while losing weight.
Yes. Endurance athletes need 1.2–1.6 g/kg, while strength athletes may need 1.6–2.2 g/kg for performance and recovery.
